title: orchis pseudosambucina subsp. sulphurea
intro: Orchis pseudosambucina subsp. sulphurea is a rare, endemic orchid species found in Greece, characterized by its yellowish-brown flowers with a reddish-brown center. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Orchis pseudosambucina subsp. sulphurea include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties.
